items.text: We go to {1} school by {2} bus.
items.explanation: 'Go to school' (as a student) and 'by bus' (means of transport) are fixed expressions with no article.

items.text: She stays at {1} home and watches {2} television.
items.explanation: 'At home' and 'watch television' take no article (contrast 'listen to the radio').

items.text: He plays {1} piano every night after {2} dinner.
items.explanation: Musical instruments take 'the' (the piano), but meals take no article (dinner).

items.text: They went to {1} bed early because they had {2} flu.
items.explanation: 'Go to bed' takes no article, but the illness 'the flu' takes 'the' (compare 'have a cold').

items.text: We had {1} lunch and then listened to {2} radio.
items.explanation: Meals take no article (lunch), but 'the radio' takes 'the' — unlike 'watch television'.
